D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Twisted-Nematic LCD Shutter

apply a voltage to untwist the liquid crystal and switch a pixel from bright to dark

With no voltage the helical liquid crystal guides the polarization through a 90° twist (Mauguin regime). Voltage stands the molecules up, the twist relaxes, and transmission collapses.

Raise the drive voltage to untwist the cell. In normally-white mode (crossed polarizers) the pixel darkens; switch to normally-black (parallel) to invert the response.
